title: Invert Binary Tree
description:
Given Invert a binary tree by swapping left and right children for every node.

Constraints:
- 0 ≤ nodes ≤ 10^5
explanation:
Intuition
Swapping the children of each node recursively mirrors the tree. The operation is local and independent per node.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: If node is None, return
2. Main Algorithm: Swap node.left and node.right
3. Return Result: Recurse on both children

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(h) recursion

Key Insights
- Purely structural transform
- Preorder or postorder both work

Common Pitfalls
- Not recursing after swap leaves subtrees uninverted
- Stack depth issues on degenerate trees
hints: Order of recursion does not matter
